prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a friendly robot detective walks along a wavy timeline ribbon holding a magnifying glass over the curve, while a tiny mischievous gremlin tugs one bump of the line and a translucent fairness scale floats overhead keeping the path level. Style friendly like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot detective inspects a wavy timeline with a magnifying glass while a gremlin nudges the curve and a fairness scale hovers above keeping it balanced.
caption: Trustworthy temporal models must be understandable, robust to tampering, and fair all along the time axis, not just at one frozen instant.

after_heading: 2. Inherently Interpretable Models versus Post-Hoc Explanation
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: an overconfident robot points a glowing spotlight at one spot on a wavy data curve as if proudly explaining its decision, but a skeptical scientist holds up a hand mirror showing the model actually looked somewhere completely different. Style like a dry XKCD panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot spotlights one point on a curve to explain itself while a scientist's mirror reveals the model truly attended elsewhere.
caption: Attention weights look like an explanation, but where a model points is not always where it actually decided.

after_heading: 2. Adversarial Examples for Time Series: FGSM and PGD
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: two nearly identical wavy signal lines sit side by side, one calm and one with a barely visible sprinkle of tiny arrows nudging it imperceptibly, yet a robot reading the second one flips from a confident smile to total panic. Style like a clever Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Two almost identical signal curves where a tiny invisible nudge on one makes a robot reader flip from calm confidence to alarm.
caption: A perturbation too small for a human to notice can flip a temporal model's prediction completely; that is the adversarial trick.

after_heading: 1. Why Fairness Has a Temporal Dimension: Sequential Decisions and Feedback Loops
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a robot waters only one of two flowerpots over and over, that plant grows huge while the neglected one wilts, and a circular pipe loops the big plant's output right back to the robot reinforcing its choice, with the robot looking pleased and oblivious. Style like a dry XKCD panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot repeatedly waters one flowerpot while a feedback pipe loops that plant's growth back to justify the choice, leaving the other pot wilting.
caption: Yesterday's biased decisions become tomorrow's training data, so an unfair loop quietly reinforces itself over time.

after_heading: 3. Federated Learning: Training Without Centralizing the Series
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: several cozy little houses each keep their own pantry of private groceries safely inside, and instead of shipping food they each send only a small floating recipe card up to a central friendly robot chef who stirs them into one shared cookbook. Style like a warm Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Houses keep private groceries inside and send only small recipe cards to a central robot chef who combines them, rather than shipping the food itself.
caption: Federated learning shares what was learned, not the raw private series; the data stays home while the model travels.

after_heading: 1. Responsible Temporal AI as a System Property
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a sturdy bridge carries a happy data stream across a gap, held up by several distinct pillars of different shapes standing together, while a robot inspector taps each pillar approvingly and one missing pillar would clearly tip the whole bridge. Style like a clear Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A bridge carrying a data stream rests on several different pillars while a robot inspector checks each one, showing the span fails if any pillar is missing.
caption: Responsibility is a property of the whole system: interpretability, robustness, fairness, and privacy are pillars that only hold together.
